info.title: Mycel API
info.description:
## Version compatibility

All requests may include the `X-Mycel-Version` header to indicate the minimum version your implementation requires (e.g. `2.3.0`). If provided, Mycel will verify compatibility and return a `version` error if incompatible.

See the implementation guide for details.

get.description: Checks that Mycel is reachable and running.
Get cloze regex used to manipulate cloze field of spores.

Returns the user settings schema dynamically.
Implementations can render a fully dynamic settings UI by parsing field types and metadata
(category, unit, step, warning, etc.) without hardcoding any specific field names or values.

post.description: Note: This route is only accessible in self-hosted deployments. It is blocked at the infrastructure level in MycelCloud, as users are created internally during account registration.

get.description:
Get the highest parent node for the given node_id.
Useful when the frontend is not using a cache and the node tree is deeply nested,
allows reaching the root without traversing multiple calls manually.

Only fragments are dismissable.

CollectionUpdate.title: CollectionUpdate
CollectionUpdate.description:
Partial update model for Collection.

Only include fields that you explicitly want to modify.
Any field provided in this model including fields set to None will be applied and will overwrite the existing value on the collection.

Fields not included in the update will remain unchanged.

DayReviewOverview.title: DayReviewOverview
DayReviewOverview.description: Not using date object to ease transfer to frontend

Node.title: Node
Node.description: Domain model gathering data related to a node and his learning units.

NodeDetailView.title: NodeDetailView
NodeDetailView.description: Full view for single node with all content.

NodeUpdate.title: NodeUpdate
NodeUpdate.description:
Partial update model for Node.

Only include fields that you explicitly want to modify.
Any field provided in this model including fields set to None will be applied and will overwrite the existing value on the node.

Fields not included in the update will remain unchanged.

NodeView.title: NodeView
NodeView.description:
Lightweight DTO for list display. Contains a short content preview with light fields to allow front side filtering.
For full field rendering, use NodeDetailView.

PlaceholderData.title: PlaceholderData
PlaceholderData.description: Placeholder class to allow discriminator in Spore model

undo_review_max_age.title: Undo Review Max Age
undo_review_max_age.description: Maximum time allowed to undo a review.

delete_max_age.title: Delete Max Age
delete_max_age.description: Number of days after which deleted nodes are permanently removed.
delete_max_age.warning: Changing this value will immediately and permanently delete nodes that have been soft-deleted for longer than the new value.

add_extract_to_nav.title: Add Extract To Nav
add_extract_to_nav.description: When extracting, insert it into the navigation history for instant access.

wait_for_due_time.title: Wait For Due Time
wait_for_due_time.description: If enabled, nodes due later in the day are never surfaced early, only nodes whose due time has already passed are reviewed.

ping_frequency.title: Ping Frequency
ping_frequency.description: When disconnected, application will attempt to reconnect to Mycel at this frequency

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description:
Partial update model for User.

Only include fields that you explicitly want to modify.
Any field provided in this model including fields set to None will be applied and will overwrite the existing value on the user.

Fields not included in the update will remain unchanged.

tags.name: nodes
tags.description: Endpoints relative to nodes. To act on learning unit-specific data, see the dedicated section. Node endpoints here only affect global node attributes, even when the node has a single learning unit (as is the case with fragments).

tags.name: learning units
tags.description:
Endpoints relative to a learning unit within a node, targeted using its slot number. Returns the fully updated node.

For common actions (such as reprioritisation), you can use dedicated endpoints. For more advanced control and precise updates, you should use the Update Learning Unit endpoint.

Endpoint names reflect the target learning unit type when the action is type-specific (e.g. dismiss_fragment rather than dismiss_learning_unit).

*If you don't want to support mutliple-learning-unit nodes, always pass 0 as the slot number.*
